summary: Downgrade plugin validation from fail-fast blocker to warning — agents may legitimately skip Twining tools
context: The fail-fast guard killed 6 valid iterations in run 16a2e4e1. Investigation proved the plugin loaded correctly (SDK test confirmed tools available), but Opus ignored the SessionStart hook instructions and coded without Twining tools. This is a behavioral finding, not an infrastructure failure — the guard was a false positive.
rationale: The agent choosing not to use available tools IS the data point. Blocking the iteration hides this signal. A warning log preserves observability while letting the run produce valid (if disappointing for Twining) results. The guard was correct to add after the Sonnet run where plugin genuinely failed to load, but it's too aggressive now that we know the plugin works.

alternatives.reason_rejected: Blocks valid iterations where agent has tools but chooses standard tools. Hides the behavioral finding that hook instructions aren't strong enough.

alternatives.option: Check plugin load at SDK level before first session
alternatives.reason_rejected: SDK doesn't expose a tool-list API pre-session. Can only observe tool usage after a session runs.
